post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 162: Bad Movie Mafia

Story
It seemed like a normal day, everyone was just hanging out and having fun.

Then e_is_cool and his assistant benjaminsz came downstairs and said to everyone else, “My interdimensional travel experiment is complete! Come on, guys, let’s go explore other dimensions!”

“Sure!”, said everyone else, and they headed upstairs to help e_is_cool test his machine. However, just as they activated it, benjaminsz accidentally leaned on the Destination dial, changing it from “Fun Adventure Dimension” to “Bad Movie Dimension”. Before they knew it, everyone had been sucked into the world’s worst movie!

The bizarre physics of that movie realm dictated that some people had to be the bad guys. So some people became the bad guys. They would forever be forced to battle everyone else, until one side emerged triumphant.

Everyone forgot that they came from another dimension and became movie characters. Only benjaminsz and e_is_cool remembered the dimension they came from, because they had built the machine that brought them here. So the gods of this movie realm struck them down and destroyed the interdimensional travel machine.

e_is_cool, the Great Inventor, also known as the Mod, has been struck by lightning from the heavens.

benjaminsz, the Inventor’s Assistant, also known as the Co-Mod, has been crushed by a NARFA (No Apparent Reason Falling Anvil).


Read all of the rules.

Voting and Lynches
  • You must vote using the format of Vote: NAME. The name must be properly spelled (with a notable exception) and everything must be formatted correctly for the vote to count.
  • You may vote for no lynch to occur by doing Vote: No Lynch.
  • Any invalid vote will be completely ignored.
  • For the purposes of voting, the letters I (captial i) and l (lowercase L) will be considered the same; this goes for any other similar looking symbols.
  • If a player receives the majority of the votes, that player will be lynched and the day will end immediately.
  • If at least half of the players vote for a No Lynch, then no lynch will occur and the day will end immediately.
  • If no one person receives the majority of the votes by the end of the day, the person with the most votes at that time will be lynched. If there are multiple such people, then the person who got to that number of votes first gets lynched.
  • There is no majority lynch during Day 1. That means a lynch will only happen at the end of the day.
  • As soon as the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted.
  • Any incorrect votecounts made will be counted as the correct votecount if the mistake is not noticed and corrected within 24 hours.


Activity and Deadlines
  • Each day will be 2 weeks long.
  • Each night will be 3 days long.
  • I can extend the deadline if I deem it necessary, but do not expect me to do so every time.
  • I will give out PM Prods at my discretion; there is no official time limit, but I may start looking for a replacement as soon as 24 hours after I give a PM Prod.
  • If you post before I find a replacement (for your inactivity), you will not be replaced.


List of Don’t’s
  • Don’t post if you are not participating in this game.
  • Don’t post at night.
  • Don’t talk about this game to anyone except me (the mod) outside of this thread, unless you are given explicit permission by me (the mod) to do so.
  • Don’t edit or delete posts.
  • Don’t conceal text with things such as transparent text and the like. Hide tags are alright to use.
  • Don’t post if you are dead.
  • Don’t use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), purple (#8000BF), or anything that resembles one of those colors.
  • Don’t be immature.
  • Don’t gamethrow.
  • Don’t argue with the mod about warnings and modkills.
  • Don’t abuse loopholes.

Violation of any of these rules will result in a warning, a force-replacement, and/or a modkill. I reserve the right to give warnings to, force-replace, and/or modkill anyone for whatever reason.

Warnings
Post #104 - Nihal - Editing Post


Setup
This game will use the Backup6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.
  • There are 9 players.
  • This setup is Day Start.
  • Town Cop is guaranteed to be sane.
  • Town Doctor CANNOT target themselves.
  • Mafia Roleblocker CAN kill and block in the same night.
  • Mafia Roleblocker’s roleblock has priority over Town Jailkeeper’s roleblock.
  • Mafia members may only discuss with each other privately at night in the designated quicktopic.
  • If you have read all of the rules and are ready to participate in the game, please make a post with the word “universal” in it.
The roles for this game will be randomly selected out of the following possible role combinations before the game starts:

1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 Town Universal Backup, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Tracker, 1 Town Universal Backup, 2 Mafia Goons

The setup that is chosen is not revealed.

Role PMs
Vanilla Townie wrote:
You are a Useless Extra Character, also known as a Vanilla Townie.

You have no night actions; your power comes from your freedom of speech and your vote.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.

You can find the game thread here.
Town Cop wrote:
You are a Sherlock Holmes Ripoff, also known as a Town Cop.

Each night, you may PM me the name of one player to inspect. You will receive a result of innocent or guilty (town or mafia), or that you got no result (you were somehow blocked).

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.

You can find the game thread here.
Town Doctor wrote:
You are a Good Doctor Stereotype, also known as a Town Doctor.

Each night you may PM me the name of a player (excluding yourself) to protect. That night, one kill targeting that player will fail (unless you were somehow blocked).

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.

You can find the game thread here.
Town Jailkeeper wrote:
You are a Lame Police Guy, also known as a Town Jailkeeper.

Each night you may PM me the name of a player to jail. Unless someone interferes, that person is roleblocked (they cannot perform any night actions) and protected (kills targeting them will fail).

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.

You can find the game thread here.
Town Tracker wrote:
You are a Token Spy Character, also known as a Town Tracker.

Each night, you may PM me the name of one player to track. You will receive the player(s) that the person targeted, if any, or that you got no result (you were somehow blocked).

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.

You can find the game thread here.
Town Universal Backup wrote:
You are a Guy Who’s Only There In Case The Writers Decide To Kill The Main Character, also known as a Town Universal Backup.

If at any point in the game a town power role (non-VT) dies, you will inherent that power role.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.

You can find the game thread here.
Mafia Goon wrote:
You are a Token Bad Guy, also known as a Mafia Goon. Your partner is NAME, a Token Bad Guy/Cheesy Supervillain, also known as a Mafia Goon/Mafia Roleblocker.

During the night, you may talk with your partner(s) in this quicktopic. Each night you or one of your partners may PM me the name of a player to kill. Unless someone interferes, on the following day, the kill will be announced.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one mafia member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.

You can find the game thread here.
Mafia Roleblocker wrote:
You are a Cheesy Supervillain, also known as a Mafia Roleblocker. Your partner is NAME, a Token Bad Guy, also known as a Mafia Goon.

During the night, you may talk with your partner(s) in this quicktopic. Each night you or one of your partners may PM me the name of a player to kill. Unless someone interferes, on the following day the kill will be announced. Each night you may also PM me the name of a player to roleblock. Unless someone interferes, that person's night action will become ineffective that night.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one mafia member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.

You can find the game thread here.

Endgame Spoilers

post_rendered:
I would highly recommend avoiding claims; while the PR's are in general not as important as the cop in Cop9, they are still very crucial, and many AoPS Newbie games have been won or lost because of them. Even if you're trying to soft PR as VT, any claim can make way for real PR's to slip up. I'd recommend against fakeclaiming altogether, too, since it's nearly impossible to pull off properly.

For the most part, though, all Mafia games are the same in the daygame. There's nothing particularly special about this setup for the daygame, and no strategy similar to the cop cover in Cop9 that would be necessary. I would say you can just play normally.
